title: Advanced Casting
subtitle: Unfocused casting, augment interactions, TTRPG options

content.text: Unfocused Casting
Casting without a focus (rift staff, channeling rod, attuned crystal) is possible but costly. Your body becomes the focus, absorbing all the channeling stress directly.
• All patterns cost +1 additional Toxicity • Misfire chance increases by +10% at all Toxicity levels • Torrent and Cataclysm patterns cost +2 additional HP
Why would you cast unfocused? Because someone broke your staff. Because you're in a prison cell. Because the creature ate your focus and you're angry. Desperation casting. It works. It hurts more.
Augment-Casting Interactions
The Rift augment tree directly enhances casting. Key augments and their effects on the casting system:

rows: Required to cast at all. Unlocks Flicker patterns.
+1 Interface accuracy on all casting rolls.
+1 damage per die on sequential single-target casts against the same target. Stacks to +3.
+5ft blast radius on sequential AoE casts that hit 2+ enemies. Stacks to +10ft.
Single-target Surge patterns cost -1 Toxicity. Rift Bolt drops to +0.
Toxicity thresholds shift up by 2. Misfire danger zone starts later.
Heal 1 HP per round when dealing rift damage. Tiny sustain.
+2 Toxicity for +1d8 bonus damage on any pattern. Once per turn.
+1 damage die on all AoE patterns. Everything hits harder.
+1 damage per 5 Toxicity on ALL attacks. High Toxicity = more damage.
Max die rolls deal that die's damage again. +2 Toxicity when triggered.
+2 accuracy and +1 damage die on ALL patterns. +1 Toxicity every round automatically.
Teleport Medium range as movement. +1 Toxicity per use.
-1 Toxicity on all patterns. Halved misfire chance. 3 Flickers/round. First Flicker free. Rift Transcendence: 0 HP cost for 2 rounds, once per encounter.

content.text: Conduit vs Storm Branch Summary

Conduit augments enhance single-target patterns: more armor penetration, damage stacking on one target, execute thresholds, sequential cast bonuses. The boss-killer branch. Every augment makes you better at deleting one creature.
Storm augments enhance AoE patterns: larger blast radii, chain kills, zone persistence, extra damage dice. The horde-clearer branch. Every augment makes you better at deleting groups.
Shared augments provide sustain, economy, and power scaling: Toxicity management, HP recovery, damage bonuses that work with both branches. The foundation that keeps you casting.
TTRPG Sandbox Options

The following systems use the 2d6 + stat resolution (10+ full / 7–9 partial / 6− failure) and are preserved for tabletop play where a GM can interpret results. These are NOT part of the video game implementation.
Improvised Channeling

Describe the effect you want. The GM assigns a tier equivalent, stat, and cost (standard for that tier + surcharge). You pay the costs and roll 2d6 + stat. 10+ = full effect, 7–9 = partial/twisted effect, 6− = misfire. Improvisation always costs more than a defined Pattern.

GMs: Be generous with improvisation. If a player describes something cool and pays the cost, make it work on a good roll. The surcharge already balances the flexibility. Improvisation is where magic feels magical.
Overchannel (Push Beyond Limits)

Announce before any cast. Double all costs (Toxicity, HP, Consequences). Effect is dramatically amplified — double damage, double area, double duration (GM determines specifics). Automatic misfire check regardless of Toxicity level. If misfire triggers: consequences are tripled instead of doubled. It's the “I don't care if it kills me” option.
Ritual Casting (Out of Combat)
Some effects are too complex for combat's pace. Ritual casting allows Torrents and Cataclysms to be cast outside combat with reduced risk and enhanced effect.
Requirements: 10 minutes of uninterrupted concentration per tier, appropriate materials (rift crystals, chalk, ambient rift energy — GM determines), casting check with advantage.
Benefits: HP cost halved (round up). Misfire table one tier lower. Effects can be enhanced (GM negotiates — wider area, longer duration, additional targets).
Costs: Full Toxicity. Full Consequences for Cataclysms. Interruption during ritual = automatic Misfire at pattern's tier + Toxicity paid.

Ritual uses: Ward a safe house. Seal a rift tear. Purify a contaminated area. Scry a distant location. Create a dimensional anchor. Enchant an item temporarily. The rift can do almost anything if you give it enough time and enough of yourself.
